title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Inspector Hakast (also referred to as Hercule Poirot) attempts to investigate the death of Ina Brent by questioning her colleagues at her office. Miss Martin, the supervisor, suggests that the office staff might have more information, as they are prone to gossiping. Hakast speaks with three typists: Janet, a blonde woman; Melanie, a dark-skinned woman; and a third smiling girl. 

The women explain that they realized something was wrong when Ina failed to return to the office at two o'clock. They had previously accompanied Ina to a courthouse, but she disappeared after that. One girl recounts learning of the death from a local shopkeeper who mentioned a girl found dead in a phone booth. The colleagues note that Ina had recently seemed preoccupied, making mistakes and acting uncharacteristically distracted. They also mention that Ina did not appear to have a boyfriend. When Hakast asks if Ina was close to Sheila Weber, the women state they were not particularly close. They inform him that Sheila Weber is currently at the Curlew Hotel with Professor Puddy.

Hakast travels to the Curlew Hotel and interrupts Professor Puddy, who is narrating a story to Hilary Weber. Despite the professor's irritation at the disturbance, Hakast insists on speaking with Sheila Weber. He notes a hint of worry in Hilary's eyes as he identifies himself and informs the professor that the matter concerns the death of Ina Brent.
